description:
Advances: specled.realized_by

Deliverable:
Add SpecLedEx.Realization.Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2 and hash_module_full_union/2 (both return {:ok, binary} | {:error, term}). Add private discover_module_exports/2 that uses Module.__info__/1 (runtime). Exclude module_info/0|1 from exports; include __struct__/0|1. Sort exports by {kind, name, arity}. Wrap in distinct envelopes ({:__module_head_union__, mod, sorted} vs {:__module_full_union__, mod, sorted}) before hashing. Source-AST fallback is NOT implemented (cold-load failures yield {:error, :not_loadable}).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: canonical_test.exs covers function-only module, function+macro module, struct module (__struct__ included), reorder-stability, envelope-tag distinction (head-union != full-union for same module), empty-public-export module deterministic, :not_loadable case, repeat-N byte-equality determinism probe.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: spec requirements specled.realized_by.bare_module_api_boundary_hash, bare_module_implementation_hash, bare_module_runtime_only_discovery, bare_module_export_filtering.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S4)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (S4/S5)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex (S5)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (no closure walk from bare modules)
- .spec/**
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/canonical.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/canonical_test.exs

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT modify the existing Canonical.hash/1 entrypoint.
- Do NOT add source-AST fallback for bare-module discovery.
- Do NOT add private functions or behaviors that invite future closure-walking from bare modules.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

- test/specled_ex/realization/canonical_test.exs (+228 / -1)

Tests added (in SpecLedEx.Realization.CanonicalTest, describe "hash_module_head_union/2 and hash_module_full_union/2"):
- function-only module produces a deterministic head-union hash
- function-only module produces a deterministic full-union hash
- function+macro module: both kinds contribute to the hash
- struct module: __struct__/0 and __struct__/1 are included in the export set
- reorder-stability: source declaration order does not affect the hash
- envelope-tag distinction: head-union != full-union for the same module
- envelope-tag distinction holds even on a degenerate (empty-export) module
- empty-public-export module: hash is deterministic
- :not_loadable: cold-load failure surfaces as {:error, :not_loadable}
- repeat-N determinism probe: byte-equal hashes across repeated calls
- module_info/0 and module_info/1 are excluded from exports
- envelope shape: head entries are {kind, name, arity, head_ast}, sorted

Verification stubs flipped: none (.spec/** is Out of Scope for this ticket; the
realized_by spec verification block stays at execute: false until a later stage
where touching .spec is allowed).

Local sanity:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors --force: clean
- mix test: 670 tests, 0 failures (full suite)
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/canonical_test.exs: 18 tests, 0 failures

Summary: Adds Canonical.hash_module_head_union/2 and hash_module_full_union/2.
Discovery is runtime-only via Module.__info__/1 (functions + macros), with
module_info/0|1 filtered out and __struct__/0|1 retained. Cold-load yields
{:error, :not_loadable}. Distinct envelope tags (:__module_head_union__ vs
:__module_full_union__) keep head/full hashes byte-distinct even on empty-
export modules. Per-export AST is pulled from BEAM debug_info and
Canonical.normalize/1-stabilized; head entries omit body, full entries
include args+guards+body. No source-AST fallback for bare modules per
specled.decision.realized_by_tier_implication. Existing Canonical.hash/1
entrypoint is unchanged. Diff touches only Allowed globs
(lib/specled_ex/realization/canonical.ex, test/specled_ex/realization/canonical_test.exs).

VERIFY: PASS
Session: agent-a1e7314925b83e4ce (parent eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991/subagents/agent-a1e7314925b83e4ce.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0, passed: 0 (the realized_by spec-verification block for these requirements stays at `execute: false`; .spec/** is Out of Scope for this ticket — flip is deferred to a later stage)
- Requirements with passing tests: 4/4 (bare_module_api_boundary_hash, bare_module_implementation_hash, bare_module_runtime_only_discovery, bare_module_export_filtering)
- Intent-level scope concerns: none — existing Canonical.hash/1 is unmodified (diff is purely additive); discovery uses Module.__info__/1 with no source-AST fallback; no private helpers walk callees/closures from bare modules
- Project verification: pass — `mix compile --warnings-as-errors --force` clean; `mix test` 670 tests, 0 failures; `mix test test/specled_ex/realization/canonical_test.exs` 18 tests, 0 failures

Source-claim trace:
- Module.__info__(:functions) and Module.__info__(:macros) are called directly on the target module (canonical.ex L235, L242)
- Code.ensure_loaded/1 gates discovery and yields {:error, :not_loadable} on failure (canonical.ex L232, L252)
- module_info/0|1 explicitly rejected (canonical.ex L237); __struct__/0|1 retained (no filter, surfaces via :functions)
- Sort key is `{kind, name, arity}` (canonical.ex L247); :function < :macro lexicographically as atoms
- Distinct envelope tags `:__module_head_union__` (L184) vs `:__module_full_union__` (L220); the empty-export degenerate-module test confirms tag-only distinction
- Per-clause AST is pulled from BEAM debug_info (post-`use`-expansion warm-runtime form) and passed through `normalize/1` — this is NOT source-AST fallback; it's the canonical post-expansion form the ADR explicitly accepts

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- `mix spec.validate` reports 31 unrelated findings, all for sibling requirements scheduled for ticket 701.5+ (silent_seed, implication_*, redundant_dup_warning, dedup_check_shared_seam, binding_ref_inferred_no_leak, bare_module_no_closure_walk, etc.). None of the four 701.4 requirements appear in the findings — the @moduletag spec list in canonical_test.exs satisfies them.
- Beyond the four S3 requirements, the test file also covers the spec scenarios this stage's hashing primitives can be exercised through (envelope-tag distinction on degenerate module, repeat-N determinism, reorder stability).
- `bare_module_no_closure_walk` is correctly NOT covered here — it's an absence-of-behavior requirement that lives at the orchestrator/closure layer (S4/S5), not at Canonical. The implementation does not introduce any closure walk seam from bare modules; the only added private helpers enumerate exports of the named module itself, never callees.
- The `head_ast_for_export` / `full_ast_for_export` helpers fall back to `:no_debug_info` (a plain atom) on debug-info extraction failure rather than a tuple — this still produces a deterministic byte-distinct hash on arity/kind/name changes (because the kind/name/arity prefix in each entry tuple still varies), and the test "function-only module produces a deterministic head-union hash" asserts byte_size 32 hashes return cleanly. Worth keeping in mind for S4: a module whose BEAM is shipped without debug_info would silently degrade to envelope-only hashing for that module's exports. Not a blocker for 701.4.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: a26339887a3e06af0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/eeca2123-30e1-430c-90c1-08dd23adf991/subagents/agent-a26339887a3e06af0.jsonl

Reasoning: Diff vs base abeee1e is 2 files, both in Allowed globs, purely additive (zero deletions; existing Canonical.hash/1 is genuinely untouched). All four S3 requirements are tagged on the @moduletag (canonical_test.exs L1-L10) and each has at least one test that fails under its primary mutation: not_loadable path (canonical.ex L232/L252) covered by the cold-module test; struct inclusion covered by struct_hash != empty_hash; head/full envelope-tag distinction covered by the degenerate-empty-module assertion (canonical.ex L172 vs L220 tags). Discovery is Module.__info__/1 only (canonical.ex L235/L242) sorted by {kind,name,arity} (L247); the BEAM debug_info read in extract_clauses is post-expansion canonical form per the ADR, NOT source-AST fallback, and no private helper walks callees from a bare module. Independent mix spec.check confirms 31 findings on stage and 39 on the abeee1e base — the 8-finding delta is exactly the four 701.4 requirements (one error + one warning each) clearing; ZERO new findings introduced. None of bare_module_api_boundary_hash, bare_module_implementation_hash, bare_module_runtime_only_discovery, bare_module_export_filtering appear in the post-stage findings. mix test on canonical_test.exs: 18/18 pass.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-701/realized-by-tier-implication. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S3: Canonical module-union hashing
